prompt: Search X for NAMED seed/Series-A AI-native startups in 2026 that sell agentic products to enterprises and have publicly discussed security/compliance/security-review friction. Who are they, what do they sell, what did they say? This profiles the actual ICP. Cite.
content:
**Several early-stage (seed/Series A) AI-native startups match this profile based on recent X discussions and announcements (primarily 2025–early 2026).** They build or sell agentic AI products (autonomous or multi-agent systems/workflows) targeted at enterprises, with a strong emphasis on or explicit discussion of security, compliance, governance, auditability, and related sales/implementation friction (e.g., shadow IT, data risks, SOC 2, GDPR, RBAC, visibility/oversight needs). Many are themselves security/governance-focused agentic platforms, which directly address the pain point for other agent builders or enterprise buyers.[[1]](https://x.com/Altimor/status/1991570999566037360)[[2]](https://x.com/bpaynews/status/2060329854130606230)

Key examples drawn from X posts (with what they sell and relevant public statements):

- **Lindy (getlindy / Flo Crivello)**: Builds no-code/text-to-agent platforms for proactive enterprise workflows (email, tasks, calendar, multi-function agents). Announced “Lindy Enterprise” features specifically to solve enterprise adoption barriers. Founder statement (Nov 2025): “Ever since I was a kid, I’ve always dreamt of one day building fully secure and compliant agentic workflows for the enterprise... IT has a mess on its hands: everyone signs up to everything, and sensitive data is flying everywhere. Lindy Enterprise ends the shadow IT nightmare. It lets companies deploy agents across all functions in a secure and compliant way. This means SSO, SCIM, RBAC, Audit Logs, and compliance with SOC2 Type II, HIPAA, and GDPR.” Also introduced Agentic Evals for monitoring/replay to prevent regressions. (Fits AI-native agentic focus; funding history includes earlier seed/Series rounds, with enterprise push around that timeframe.)[[1]](https://x.com/Altimor/status/1991570999566037360)

- **Geordie**: AI agent security/governance platform for enterprises, with real-time activity tracking, governance, and risk capping for autonomous agents. Closed $30M Series A (led by Balderton et al.). Positioned squarely in the “enterprise AI agent security” category that emerged rapidly.[[2]](https://x.com/bpaynews/status/2060329854130606230)

- **Surf AI**: Agentic operations/remediation layer for enterprise security teams (context graph across identity/cloud/HR/IT/security tools; specialized AI agents for coordinated action with human oversight). Emerged with $40M Series A (total ~$57M; Accel-led). Highlights execution gaps beyond detection: “Security teams do not mainly have a detection issue anymore. They have an execution issue.”[[3]](https://x.com/GrishinRobotics/status/2034212666164629849)

- **Lema AI**: Agentic AI security platform focused on enterprise supply chain/third-party risk management. Emerged from stealth with $24M Series A (Team8-led).[[4]](https://x.com/pulse2news/status/2021951008541426050)

- **Lumia**: AI security & governance platform providing context-aware oversight, adaptive policies, and visibility for agentic AI without endpoint changes. Closed $18M seed (Team8-led; advisory includes former NSA Director). Explicitly tied to “the rise of agentic AI.”[[5]](https://x.com/menlotimes/status/1996845751394357420)

- **Furl**: Security remediation tools/platform for agentic AI. Raised $10M seed.[[6]](https://x.com/byul_finance/status/2013976804244230221)

- **Prime Security**: Agentic AI security-focused startup. Raised $20M Series A.[[7]](https://x.com/EpicPlain/status/1998433349728092411)

- **AppFactor**: Agentic orchestration platform that automates enterprise software maintenance/workflows. Raised $4M seed.[[8]](https://x.com/ITSecurityWire/status/2019042879591895436)

**Additional context from X (broader trend, not single startups)**: Posts reference an “Enterprise AI Agent Security Crisis,” rapid emergence of agent security as a Series A category (e.g., Onyx Security $40M raise noted alongside malicious skills findings and bans), and the need for governance/oversight as adoption outpaces controls. Security startups in this space are explicitly building to solve the friction that general agentic startups encounter when selling to enterprises (audits, data control, compliance, visibility).[[9]](https://x.com/MindTheGapMTG/status/2035337605458338165)[[10]](https://x.com/ai_news_flash_/status/2025832327897817518)

**ICP profile summary**: These are typically 2023–2025 founded AI-native teams (often with cybersecurity or enterprise SaaS backgrounds) raising seed-to-Series A in 2025–2026. They target mid-market to large enterprises with agentic products but must prioritize (or build around) enterprise-grade controls—SSO/SCIM/RBAC, audit logs, SOC 2/HIPAA/GDPR, data residency/residency concerns, risk capping, and observability—to overcome sales friction like shadow IT fears or lengthy security reviews. The rise of dedicated “agent security/governance” agentic platforms underscores this as a core buyer requirement and emerging sub-category. Many announcements on X tie funding directly to solving these exact issues.

Note: Exact current stage/funding can evolve quickly; the above are based on contemporaneous X posts and announcements from the relevant period. Some pure-play security agent startups (e.g., Geordie, Lumia) are particularly strong fits as they sell agentic solutions *to address* the friction.
